symlink-alias: A symlink alias resolves to the same project
nested-path: A deep subdirectory resolves to the repository root
nested-repository: A nested independent repository is its own project
submodule: A submodule is its own project with its own repo family
linked-worktree: A user linked worktree is a distinct Hive that shares a repo family
separate-clone: A separate clone is a distinct Hive with a distinct repo family
bare-repository: A bare repository is refused
inside-git-dir: The .git directory is not a project root
git-env-hijack: A hostile GIT_DIR in the environment cannot redirect discovery
rename: Renaming a project root requires a confirmed rebind
move-across-parents: Moving a project to a different parent directory requires a rebind
move-then-impostor: A fresh directory at a moved project's old path never inherits it
delete-recreate: A deleted and recreated path never inherits the old identity
plain-directory: A plain directory uses its exact canonical root
use-parent: Use Parent is ephemeral: legal before the child is registered, refused after
managed-worktree-ledger: Managed-worktree ownership comes from the authenticated ledger, never from a repo file
concurrent-starts: Twenty simultaneous starts for one root yield one HiveUUID
bookmark-path-first: Plain Foundation bookmarks resolve path-first, not file-ID-first
inode-reuse: APFS does not reuse directory inode numbers across delete/create
case-insensitive-volume: On a case-insensitive volume, Foo and foo are one project
case-sensitive-volume: On a case-sensitive volume, Foo and foo are two projects
cross-volume-move: A cross-volume move is a copy and must not inherit the old identity
